pattern: A same-value path bends at one node, so each node returns the longest single-direction equal-value arm to its parent while the answer tracks the best left-plus-right combination.
hints: Only a child whose value equals its parent can extend a same-value run downward.
At each node, a downward arm continues through a child only when that child shares the node's value; otherwise the arm length resets to zero.
The longest path centered at a node joins its left equal arm and right equal arm; return only the longer single arm upward so the parent can extend it.

edge_inputs.description: all equal so longest path spans the whole tree shape
path bends at the root joining two equal arms

stress.description: random tree with a small value alphabet to force frequent equal runs
stress.note: small alphabet makes univalue paths common
